opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The pain is tearing through my whole pelvis and lower back and nothing I take is dulling the agony. It came on suddenly last night and now I can't even stand up. My partner is here and we're really scared.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It suddenly got unbearable about 12 hours ago, I've been throwing up and I can't get out of bed — my partner made me call.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about 12 hours ago, last night
symptom_narrative.duration: around 12 hours and getting worse the whole time
symptom_narrative.character: started as a dull ache then turned into a sharp throbbing pain all through my pelvis
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's ever been, absolute agony, nothing touches it
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all through my pelvis and lower back, and it spreads down both my thighs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, just building and building
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing at all, I've taken everything I've got
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving makes it worse, and it spikes really badly when my bladder fills up or when I go to the toilet
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel really sick and I've been vomiting, and I'm completely wiped out
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get bad period pain and pelvic pain from my endometriosis, but it has never, ever been this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't get out of bed or walk to the bathroom, I'm just curled up in a ball

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her partner, who is with her and very worried

objective_data_offered.value: she looks really pale and sweaty and is curled up in bed, she can't stand up
I keep being sick and can't really keep anything down
my heart feels like it's racing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective sensation, no device reading offered

objective_data_offered.value: there are heat packs and a load of empty over-the-counter painkiller packets on the bed next to her
